3	import { Toggle as TogglePrimitive } from "@base-ui/react/toggle"
4	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
5	
6	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
7	
8	const toggleVariants = cva(
9	  "group/toggle inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-1 rounded-lg text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none hover:bg-muted hover:text-foreground focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 aria-invalid:border-destructive aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 aria-pressed:bg-muted data-[state=on]:bg-muted dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
10	  {
11	    variants: {
12	      variant: {
13	        default: "bg-transparent",
14	        outline: "border border-input bg-transparent hover:bg-muted",
15	      },
16	      size: {
17	        default:
18	          "h-8 min-w-8 px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-2 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-2",
19	        sm: "h-7 min-w-7 rounded-[min(var(--radius-md),12px)] px-2.5 text-[0.8rem] has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-1.5 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3.5",
20	        lg: "h-9 min-w-9 px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-2 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-2",
21	      },
22	    },
23	    defaultVariants: {
24	      variant: "default",
25	      size: "default",
26	    },
27	  }
28	)
29	
30	function Toggle({
31	  className,
32	  variant = "default",
33	  size = "default",
34	  ...props
35	}: TogglePrimitive.Props & VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
36	  return (
37	    <TogglePrimitive
38	      data-slot="toggle"
39	      className={cn(toggleVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
40	      {...props}
41	    />
42	  )
43	}
44	
45	export { Toggle, toggleVariants }
46

1	"use client"

3	import * as React from "react"
4	import { Slider as SliderPrimitive } from "@base-ui/react/slider"
5	
6	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
7	
8	function Slider({
9	  className,
10	  defaultValue,
11	  value,
12	  min = 0,
13	  max = 100,
14	  ...props
15	}: SliderPrimitive.Root.Props) {
16	  const _values = React.useMemo(
17	    () =>
18	      Array.isArray(value)
19	        ? value
20	        : Array.isArray(defaultValue)
21	          ? defaultValue
22	          : [min, max],
23	    [value, defaultValue, min, max]
24	  )
25	
26	  return (
27	    <SliderPrimitive.Root
28	      className={cn("data-horizontal:w-full data-vertical:h-full", className)}
29	      data-slot="slider"
30	      defaultValue={defaultValue}
31	      value={value}
32	      min={min}
33	      max={max}
34	      thumbAlignment="edge"
35	      {...props}
36	    >
37	      <SliderPrimitive.Control className="relative flex w-full touch-none items-center select-none data-disabled:opacity-50 data-vertical:h-full data-vertical:min-h-40 data-vertical:w-auto data-vertical:flex-col">
38	        <SliderPrimitive.Track
39	          data-slot="slider-track"
40	          className="relative grow overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-muted select-none data-horizontal:h-1 data-horizontal:w-full data-vertical:h-full data-vertical:w-1"
41	        >
42	          <SliderPrimitive.Indicator
43	            data-slot="slider-range"
44	            className="bg-primary select-none data-horizontal:h-full data-vertical:w-full"
45	          />
46	        </SliderPrimitive.Track>
47	        {Array.from({ length: _values.length }, (_, index) => (
48	          <SliderPrimitive.Thumb
49	            data-slot="slider-thumb"
50	            key={index}
51	            className="relative block size-3 shrink-0 rounded-full border border-ring bg-white ring-ring/50 transition-[color,box-shadow] select-none after:absolute after:-inset-2 hover:ring-3 focus-visible:ring-3 focus-visible:outline-hidden active:ring-3 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"
52	          />
53	        ))}
54	      </SliderPrimitive.Control>
55	    </SliderPrimitive.Root>
56	  )
57	}
58	
59	export { Slider }

3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),

115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({

123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({

129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([

140

145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.

156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.

177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [

184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({

187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.

200

206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(

217

222

263

1	import { mergeProps } from "@base-ui/react/merge-props"
2	import { useRender } from "@base-ui/react/use-render"
3	import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
4	
5	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
6	import { Separator } from "@/styles/base-nova/ui/separator"
7	
8	const buttonGroupVariants = cva(
9	  "flex w-fit items-stretch *:focus-visible:relative *:focus-visible:z-10 has-[>[data-slot=button-group]]:gap-2 has-[select[aria-hidden=true]:last-child]:[&>[data-slot=select-trigger]:last-of-type]:rounded-r-lg [&>[data-slot=select-trigger]:not([class*='w-'])]:w-fit [&>input]:flex-1",
10	  {
11	    variants: {
12	      orientation: {
13	        horizontal:
14	          "*:data-slot:rounded-r-none [&>[data-slot]:not(:has(~[data-slot]))]:rounded-r-lg! [&>[data-slot]~[data-slot]]:rounded-l-none [&>[data-slot]~[data-slot]]:border-l-0",
15	        vertical:
16	          "flex-col *:data-slot:rounded-b-none [&>[data-slot]:not(:has(~[data-slot]))]:rounded-b-lg! [&>[data-slot]~[data-slot]]:rounded-t-none [&>[data-slot]~[data-slot]]:border-t-0",
17	      },
18	    },
19	    defaultVariants: {
20	      orientation: "horizontal",
21	    },
22	  }
23	)
24	
25	function ButtonGroup({
26	  className,
27	  orientation,
28	  ...props
29	}: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & VariantProps<typeof buttonGroupVariants>) {
30	  return (
31	    <div
32	      role="group"
33	      data-slot="button-group"
34	      data-orientation={orientation}
35	      className={cn(buttonGroupVariants({ orientation }), className)}
36	      {...props}
37	    />
38	  )
39	}
40	
41	function ButtonGroupText({
42	  className,
43	  render,
44	  ...props
45	}: useRender.ComponentProps<"div">) {
46	  return useRender({
47	    defaultTagName: "div",
48	    props: mergeProps<"div">(
49	      {
50	        className: cn(
51	          "flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border bg-muted px-2.5 text-sm font-medium [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
52	          className
53	        ),
54	      },
55	      props
56	    ),
57	    render,
58	    state: {
59	      slot: "button-group-text",
60	    },
61	  })
62	}
63	
64	function ButtonGroupSeparator({
65	  className,
66	  orientation = "vertical",
67	  ...props
68	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof Separator>) {
69	  return (
70	    <Separator
71	      data-slot="button-group-separator"
72	      orientation={orientation}
73	      className={cn(
74	        "relative self-stretch bg-input data-horizontal:mx-px data-horizontal:w-auto data-vertical:my-px data-vertical:h-auto",
75	        className
76	      )}
77	      {...props}
78	    />
79	  )
80	}
81	
82	export {
83	  ButtonGroup,
84	  ButtonGroupSeparator,
85	  ButtonGroupText,
86	  buttonGroupVariants,
87	}

24	/*
25	 * build-registry.mts is the single v4 registry pipeline.
26	 *
27	 * Source of truth:
28	 * - Authored raw component/registry source lives in registry/bases/base and
29	 *   registry/bases/radix.
30	 * - Authored demo source lives in examples/base and examples/radix.
31	 * - Style tokens live in registry/styles/style-*.css.
32	 *
33	 * Persistent outputs:
34	 * - registry/bases/__index__.tsx
35	 * - registry/__index__.tsx
36	 * - examples/__index__.tsx
37	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui/*
38	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
39	 * - public/r/*
40	 *
41	 * Temporary outputs:
42	 * - registry/<base-style>/*
43	 * - registry-<style>.json
44	 *
45	 * Execution order:
46	 * 1. Build registry/bases/__index__.tsx from the authored base registries.
47	 * 2. Build temporary styled registries under registry/<base-style>.
48	 * 3. Build registry/__index__.tsx for runtime lookup across legacy styles and
49	 *    generated base-style combinations.
50	 * 4. Build examples/__index__.tsx from authored demos.
51	 * 5. Export public/r/* for every style through the shadcn CLI.
52	 * 6. Copy compiled ui/* from the temporary registries into styles/<style>/ui.
53	 * 7. Build styles/<style>/ui-rtl for base-nova and radix-nova only.
54	 * 8. Format the generated persistent outputs.
55	 * 9. Clean up the temporary registry/<base-style> trees and registry-*.json.
56	 */

Perfect! I now have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings:

## Summary: shadcn/ui Registry System

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui registry system. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of how components and examples are registered:

### Key Files and Locations

**Schema & Type Definitions:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts` - The core Zod schema definitions
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json` - JSON Schema for registry items
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/public/schema/registry.json` - JSON Schema for the complete registry

**Main Registry Modules:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/lib/registry.ts` - Runtime registry loader with caching
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` - Main style registry aggregator

**Component & Example Registries:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - UI component registrations
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - Example registrations

**Build System:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` - Main build pipeline

### Registration Format

#### RegistryItem Schema (Core Structure)

```typescript
// Required fields
- name: string              // Unique identifier for the item
- type: enum               // One of the registry types below

// Optional metadata
- title?: string           // Human-readable title
- description?: string     // Brief description
- author?: string          // Author name/contact
- categories?: string[]    // Categorization tags

// Dependencies
- dependencies?: string[]         // NPM packages required
- devDependencies?: string[]      // NPM dev packages
- registryDependencies?: string[] // Other registry items this depends on

// Files (the actual component code)
- files: Array<{
    path: string                  // Source file path
    type: string                  // File type (must match or specify different type)
    target?: string              // Where to install in user project
    content?: string             // Loaded at runtime
  }>

// Configuration
- tailwind?: { config?: {...} }  // Tailwind config additions
- cssVars?: {                     // CSS variable mappings
    theme?: Record<string, string>
    light?: Record<string, string>
    dark?: Record<string, string>
  }
- css?: {...}                     // Raw CSS to add
- envVars?: Record<string, string> // Environment variables

// Documentation
- docs?: string            // Markdown documentation
- meta?: any               // Custom metadata
```

#### Registry Types

```
registry:ui          - UI component (like button, accordion)
registry:component   - Complex component/block
registry:block       - Multi-component block
registry:example     - Example/demo of a component
registry:lib        - Utility library
registry:hook       - React hook
registry:theme      - Theme configuration
registry:style      - Style configuration
registry:page       - Full page component
registry:file       - Raw file to copy
registry:base       - Base style definition
registry:font       - Font configuration
registry:item       - Generic item
registry:internal   - Internal use only
```

### Concrete Example: Accordion + Example

**Component Registration (UI Registry):**
File: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],  // NPM dependency
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Component File:**
File: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`
- Exports: `Accordion`, `AccordionItem`, `AccordionTrigger`, `AccordionContent`
- Uses Radix UI primitives with custom styling via CVA (class-variance-authority)
- Includes Tailwind classes for responsive design

**Example Registration (Examples Registry):**
File: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["accordion"],  // Links to the accordion component
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Example File:**
File: `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/accordion-demo.tsx`
- Imports the accordion component from the registry
- Provides a working demo showing how to use the component
- Exports a default function component

### Separate Registries

The system uses multiple separate registries organized by function:

1. **UI Registry** (`ui/_registry.ts`) - Base UI components
2. **Examples Registry** (`examples/_registry.ts`) - Examples and demos
3. **Blocks Registry** (`blocks/_registry.ts`) - Multi-component blocks
4. **Hooks Registry** (`hooks/_registry.ts`) - React hooks
5. **Lib Registry** (`lib/_registry.ts`) - Utilities and helpers
6. **Internal Registry** (`internal/_registry.ts`) - Internal-only items
7. **Charts Registry** (`charts/_registry.ts`) - Chart components

All are imported and merged in the main `registry.ts` file.

### Auto-Generated Index Files

The system generates optimized index files (autogenerated, do not edit):

1. **`/apps/v4/registry/__index__.tsx`** - Component lookup index with lazy-loaded components
2. **`/apps/v4/examples/__index__.tsx`** - Example lookup index with lazy-loaded demos
3. **`/apps/v4/registry/bases/__index__.tsx`** - Base style registry index

These are created by `build-registry.mts` and include React.lazy() wrapped components for optimal loading.

### Key Insights

1. **Separate UI vs Examples**: Components are registered separately from examples, allowing multiple examples per component
2. **Lazy Loading**: Components use `React.lazy()` for code splitting
3. **Import Rewriting**: The `fixImport()` function normalizes cross-registry imports for users
4. **File Type Flexibility**: Files can be registered as different types; default targets are inferred from type
5. **Caching**: Runtime registry loader uses LRU cache (5-minute TTL) for performance
6. **Multi-Style Support**: Same component can exist across different style combinations (e.g., `new-york-v4`, `radix-luma`, etc.)
7. **Registry Dependencies**: Components can depend on other registry items, creating a dependency graph

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the findings into a detailed report.

## Summary of Compound Components in shadcn/ui

I've analyzed the shadcn/ui codebase and examined several key compound components that demonstrate React context patterns and modern component composition. Here are the detailed findings:

---

### 1. **Toggle Group Component** (Most Educational for Context Usage)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/toggle-group.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Context Creation with TypeScript:** Uses `React.createContext` with a typed interface
- **Custom Hook:** `ToggleGroupContext` provides variant props + custom properties (spacing, orientation)
- **Provider Pattern:** Wraps children with context value at parent component
- **Consumer Pattern:** Sub-components (`ToggleGroupItem`) use `React.useContext()` to consume context

```typescript
// Context with typed props
const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
    spacing?: number
    orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
  }
>({...})

// Parent component
function ToggleGroup({
  variant, size, spacing = 0, orientation = "horizontal", children, ...props
}) {
  return (
    <ToggleGroupPrimitive data-slot="toggle-group" data-orientation={orientation} ...>
      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing, orientation }}>
        {children}
      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
    </ToggleGroupPrimitive>
  )
}

// Sub-component
function ToggleGroupItem({ variant, size, ...props }) {
  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
  return (
    <TogglePrimitive
      className={cn(
        toggleVariants({
          variant: context.variant || variant,
          size: context.size || size,
        }),
        ...
      )}
    />
  )
}
```

**Exports:** `export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }`

---

### 2. **Accordion Component** (Simple Wrapper Pattern)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **No context usage** - relies on Base UI primitives for state management
- **Direct wrapping of primitives:** `AccordionPrimitive.Root`, `.Item`, `.Trigger`, `.Panel`
- **Icon state management via CSS:** Uses `group-aria-expanded` to toggle icons
- **Slot-based styling:** Uses `data-slot` attributes for targeted CSS

**Sub-components:**
- `Accordion` (wraps Root)
- `AccordionItem` (wraps Item)
- `AccordionTrigger` (wraps Header + Trigger)
- `AccordionContent` (wraps Panel)

**Imports:** `from "@base-ui/react/accordion"`
**Styling Utilities:** `cn()`, animation classes (`animate-accordion-down`, `animate-accordion-up`)

---

### 3. **Tabs Component** (Orientation Support with CVA)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **CVA (Class Variance Authority) for variants:**
  ```typescript
  const tabsListVariants = cva(
    "group/tabs-list inline-flex ... group-data-horizontal/tabs:h-8 group-data-vertical/tabs:flex-col",
    {
      variants: {
        variant: { default: "bg-muted", line: "gap-1 bg-transparent" },
      },
      defaultVariants: { variant: "default" },
    }
  )
  ```
- **Data attributes for orientation:** `data-horizontal` / `data-vertical`
- **Group-based selectors:** Parent sets `group/tabs`, children use `group-data-*` selectors
- **No context** - parent passes orientation via data attributes

**Sub-components:**
- `Tabs` (Root with orientation)
- `TabsList` (with variant CVA)
- `TabsTrigger` (with active state styling)
- `TabsContent` (simple panel wrapper)

**Exports:** `export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants }`

---

### 4. **Sidebar Component** (Complex Context with State Management)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/sidebar.tsx`

**Key Patterns - Most Complex:**
- **Rich Context Type Definition:**
  ```typescript
  type SidebarContextProps = {
    state: "expanded" | "collapsed"
    open: boolean
    setOpen: (open: boolean) => void
    openMobile: boolean
    setOpenMobile: (open: boolean) => void
    isMobile: boolean
    toggleSidebar: () => void
  }
  
  const SidebarContext = React.createContext<SidebarContextProps | null>(null)
  ```

- **Custom Hook with Error Handling:**
  ```typescript
  function useSidebar() {
    const context = React.useContext(SidebarContext)
    if (!context) {
      throw new Error("useSidebar must be used within a SidebarProvider.")
    }
    return context
  }
  ```

- **Provider with Controlled/Uncontrolled Pattern:**
  - Supports both `open` prop (controlled) and `defaultOpen` prop (uncontrolled)
  - Uses `React.useCallback` and `React.useMemo` for optimization
  - Persistent state via cookies

- **Data Attributes for State:**
  - `data-state="expanded" | "collapsed"`
  - `data-collapsible="offcanvas" | "icon" | "none"`
  - `data-variant="sidebar" | "floating" | "inset"`
  - `data-side="left" | "right"`

- **Sub-components use context:**
  ```typescript
  function SidebarTrigger({ className, onClick, ...props }) {
    const { toggleSidebar } = useSidebar()
    return <Button onClick={(event) => { onClick?.(event); toggleSidebar() }} />
  }
  ```

- **CVA for Menu Button Variants:**
  ```typescript
  const sidebarMenuButtonVariants = cva(
    "peer/menu-button ... group-data-[collapsible=icon]:size-8! ...",
    {
      variants: {
        variant: { default: "...", outline: "..." },
        size: { default: "h-8", sm: "h-7", lg: "h-12" },
      },
    }
  )
  ```

**20+ Sub-components:** `SidebarTrigger`, `SidebarRail`, `SidebarInset`, `SidebarHeader`, `SidebarContent`, `SidebarMenu`, `SidebarMenuItem`, `SidebarMenuButton`, `SidebarMenuAction`, `SidebarMenuBadge`, `SidebarMenuSkeleton`, `SidebarMenuSub`, `SidebarMenuSubButton`, etc.

---

### 5. **Button Group Component** (Orientation Variant Pattern)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/button-group.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Orientation variants in CVA:**
  ```typescript
  const buttonGroupVariants = cva(
    "flex w-fit items-stretch ... has-[>[data-slot=button-group]]:gap-2 ...",
    {
      variants: {
        orientation: {
          horizontal: "*:data-slot:rounded-r-none [&>[data-slot]:not(:has(~[data-slot]))]:rounded-r-lg! ...",
          vertical: "flex-col *:data-slot:rounded-b-none ...",
        },
      },
      defaultVariants: { orientation: "horizontal" },
    }
  )
  ```
- **Data attribute storage:** `data-orientation={orientation}`
- **Advanced CSS selectors:** Uses `:has()`, `:not()`, `~[data-slot]` combinators
- **useRender pattern:** Uses Base UI's `useRender` for render prop support

---

### 6. **Card Component** (Semantic Composition)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/card.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Size variant via data attribute (no CVA):**
  ```typescript
  function Card({ className, size = "default", ...props }) {
    return (
      <div data-slot="card" data-size={size} className={cn("... data-[size=sm]:gap-3 ...")} />
    )
  }
  ```
- **Semantic sub-components:** `CardHeader`, `CardTitle`, `CardDescription`, `CardAction`, `CardContent`, `CardFooter`
- **Group parent styling:** Children use `group-data-[size=sm]/card:` selectors
- **No context** - uses CSS custom properties and data attributes

**Export Pattern:** 7 named exports

---

### 7. **Navigation Menu Component** (Portal + Positioning)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/navigation-menu.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **CVA for trigger styling:**
  ```typescript
  const navigationMenuTriggerStyle = cva(
    "group/navigation-menu-trigger ... data-popup-open:bg-muted/50 ..."
  )
  ```
- **Portal pattern:** Uses `NavigationMenuPrimitive.Portal` and `Positioner`
- **Icon rotation on open:** `group-data-popup-open/navigation-menu-trigger:rotate-180`
- **Viewport composition:** Nested viewport within popup

---

### **Utility Patterns Used Across All Components:**

1. **cn() Function** (utility merger):
   ```typescript
   // File: /apps/v4/lib/utils.ts
   export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
     return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
   }
   ```
   Uses `clsx` for conditional classes + `tailwind-merge` for conflict resolution

2. **CVA (Class Variance Authority) Pattern:**
   ```typescript
   import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
   const myVariants = cva("base", { variants: {...} })
   // Then use: VariantProps<typeof myVariants> in function signatures
   ```

3. **Data Slot Attributes:**
   - Every component adds `data-slot="component-name"`
   - Enables CSS targeting without class dependencies
   - Child selectors use `**:data-[slot=child-name]`

4. **Group/Namespace Selectors:**
   - Parent: `group/toggle-group` or `group/tabs`
   - Children: `group-data-[variant=line]/tabs-list:...` or `group-aria-expanded/accordion-trigger:...`

5. **Base UI Primitives:**
   - All components wrap `@base-ui/react/*` primitives
   - Direct type inheritance: `AccordionPrimitive.Root.Props`

6. **useRender Pattern** (from Base UI):
   ```typescript
   useRender({
     defaultTagName: "div",
     props: mergeProps<"div">(baseProps, props),
     render, // optional render prop
     state: { slot: "...", ... },
   })
   ```

7. **Context with Fallbacks:**
   - Context providers have default values
   - Sub-components can work with or without context
   - Context values default to parent's variant/size if provided

---

### **Export Patterns:**

- **Compound components export as named exports only:**
  ```typescript
  export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }
  export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants }
  export { Sidebar, SidebarContent, ..., useSidebar } // 24+ exports
  ```

- **No default exports** - supports tree-shaking
- **Sometimes export CVA variants** for external use: `tabsListVariants`, `sidebarMenuButtonVariants`
- **Export custom hooks** when applicable: `useSidebar`

---

This structure demonstrates modern React component composition with excellent separation of concerns, accessibility, and styling flexibility.

- **No context** - parent passes orientation via data attributes

**Sub-components:**
- `Tabs` (Root with orientation)
- `TabsList` (with variant CVA)
- `TabsTrigger` (with active state styling)
- `TabsContent` (simple panel wrapper)

**Exports:** `export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants }`

---

### 4. **Sidebar Component** (Complex Context with State Management)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/sidebar.tsx`

**Key Patterns - Most Complex:**
- **Rich Context Type Definition:**
  ```typescript
  type SidebarContextProps = {
    state: "expanded" | "collapsed"
    open: boolean
    setOpen: (open: boolean) => void
    openMobile: boolean
    setOpenMobile: (open: boolean) => void
    isMobile: boolean
    toggleSidebar: () => void
  }
  
  const SidebarContext = React.createContext<SidebarContextProps | null>(null)
  ```

- **Custom Hook with Error Handling:**
  ```typescript
  function useSidebar() {
    const context = React.useContext(SidebarContext)
    if (!context) {
      throw new Error("useSidebar must be used within a SidebarProvider.")
    }
    return context
  }
  ```

- **Provider with Controlled/Uncontrolled Pattern:**
  - Supports both `open` prop (controlled) and `defaultOpen` prop (uncontrolled)
  - Uses `React.useCallback` and `React.useMemo` for optimization
  - Persistent state via cookies

- **Data Attributes for State:**
  - `data-state="expanded" | "collapsed"`
  - `data-collapsible="offcanvas" | "icon" | "none"`
  - `data-variant="sidebar" | "floating" | "inset"`
  - `data-side="left" | "right"`

- **Sub-components use context:**
  ```typescript
  function SidebarTrigger({ className, onClick, ...props }) {
    const { toggleSidebar } = useSidebar()
    return <Button onClick={(event) => { onClick?.(event); toggleSidebar() }} />
  }
  ```

- **CVA for Menu Button Variants:**
  ```typescript
  const sidebarMenuButtonVariants = cva(
    "peer/menu-button ... group-data-[collapsible=icon]:size-8! ...",
    {
      variants: {
        variant: { default: "...", outline: "..." },
        size: { default: "h-8", sm: "h-7", lg: "h-12" },
      },
    }
  )
  ```

**20+ Sub-components:** `SidebarTrigger`, `SidebarRail`, `SidebarInset`, `SidebarHeader`, `SidebarContent`, `SidebarMenu`, `SidebarMenuItem`, `SidebarMenuButton`, `SidebarMenuAction`, `SidebarMenuBadge`, `SidebarMenuSkeleton`, `SidebarMenuSub`, `SidebarMenuSubButton`, etc.

---

### 5. **Button Group Component** (Orientation Variant Pattern)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/button-group.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Orientation variants in CVA:**
  ```typescript
  const buttonGroupVariants = cva(
    "flex w-fit items-stretch ... has-[>[data-slot=button-group]]:gap-2 ...",
    {
      variants: {
        orientation: {
          horizontal: "*:data-slot:rounded-r-none [&>[data-slot]:not(:has(~[data-slot]))]:rounded-r-lg! ...",
          vertical: "flex-col *:data-slot:rounded-b-none ...",
        },
      },
      defaultVariants: { orientation: "horizontal" },
    }
  )
  ```
- **Data attribute storage:** `data-orientation={orientation}`
- **Advanced CSS selectors:** Uses `:has()`, `:not()`, `~[data-slot]` combinators
- **useRender pattern:** Uses Base UI's `useRender` for render prop support

---

### 6. **Card Component** (Semantic Composition)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/card.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Size variant via data attribute (no CVA):**
  ```typescript
  function Card({ className, size = "default", ...props }) {
    return (
      <div data-slot="card" data-size={size} className={cn("... data-[size=sm]:gap-3 ...")} />
    )
  }
  ```
- **Semantic sub-components:** `CardHeader`, `CardTitle`, `CardDescription`, `CardAction`, `CardContent`, `CardFooter`
- **Group parent styling:** Children use `group-data-[size=sm]/card:` selectors
- **No context** - uses CSS custom properties and data attributes

**Export Pattern:** 7 named exports

---

### 7. **Navigation Menu Component** (Portal + Positioning)
**File Path:** `/apps/v4/styles/base-nova/ui/navigation-menu.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **CVA for trigger styling:**
  ```typescript
  const navigationMenuTriggerStyle = cva(
    "group/navigation-menu-trigger ... data-popup-open:bg-muted/50 ..."
  )
  ```
- **Portal pattern:** Uses `NavigationMenuPrimitive.Portal` and `Positioner`
- **Icon rotation on open:** `group-data-popup-open/navigation-menu-trigger:rotate-180`
- **Viewport composition:** Nested viewport within popup

---

### **Utility Patterns Used Across All Components:**

1. **cn() Function** (utility merger):
   ```typescript
   // File: /apps/v4/lib/utils.ts
   export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
     return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
   }
   ```
   Uses `clsx` for conditional classes + `tailwind-merge` for conflict resolution

2. **CVA (Class Variance Authority) Pattern:**
   ```typescript
   import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
   const myVariants = cva("base", { variants: {...} })
   // Then use: VariantProps<typeof myVariants> in function signatures
   ```

3. **Data Slot Attributes:**
   - Every component adds `data-slot="component-name"`
   - Enables CSS targeting without class dependencies
   - Child selectors use `**:data-[slot=child-name]`

4. **Group/Namespace Selectors:**
   - Parent: `group/toggle-group` or `group/tabs`
   - Children: `group-data-[variant=line]/tabs-list:...` or `group-aria-expanded/accordion-trigger:...`

5. **Base UI Primitives:**
   - All components wrap `@base-ui/react/*` primitives
   - Direct type inheritance: `AccordionPrimitive.Root.Props`

6. **useRender Pattern** (from Base UI):
   ```typescript
   useRender({
     defaultTagName: "div",
     props: mergeProps<"div">(baseProps, props),
     render, // optional render prop
     state: { slot: "...", ... },
   })
   ```

7. **Context with Fallbacks:**
   - Context providers have default values
   - Sub-components can work with or without context
   - Context values default to parent's variant/size if provided

---

### **Export Patterns:**

- **Compound components export as named exports only:**
  ```typescript
  export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }
  export { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent, tabsListVariants }
  export { Sidebar, SidebarContent, ..., useSidebar } // 24+ exports
  ```

- **No default exports** - supports tree-shaking
- **Sometimes export CVA variants** for external use: `tabsListVariants`, `sidebarMenuButtonVariants`
- **Export custom hooks** when applicable: `useSidebar`

---

This structure demonstrates modern React component composition with excellent separation of concerns, accessibility, and styling flexibility.

10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number

16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})

20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(

70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),

73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >

import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( Product Information

Our flagship product combines cutting-edge technology with sleek design. Built with premium materials, it offers unparalleled performance and reliability.

Key features include advanced processing capabilities, and an intuitive user interface designed for both beginners and experts.

Shipping Details

We offer worldwide shipping through trusted courier partners. Standard delivery takes 3-5 business days, while express shipping ensures delivery within 1-2 business days.

All orders are carefully packaged and fully insured. Track your shipment in real-time through our dedicated tracking portal.

Return Policy

We stand behind our products with a comprehensive 30-day return policy. If you're not completely satisfied, simply return the item in its original condition.

Our hassle-free return process includes free return shipping and full refunds processed within 48 hours of receiving the returned item.

) }

Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in the shadcn/ui component library. ## Context This is the shadcn/ui monorepo. The component library lives in: - **UI components**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` (e.g., `tabs.tsx`, `carousel.tsx`, `toggle-group.tsx`) - **Examples**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` (e.g., `accordion-demo.tsx`) - **UI registry**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - **Examples registry**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - **Main registry**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` (imports from sub-registries, no changes needed here since it already spreads `...ui` and `...examples`) ## Requirements Create a **Stepper** compound component for multi-step wizards/onboarding flows. It must: 1. **Sub-components**: `Stepper` (container), `StepperItem` (individual step), `StepperTrigger` (clickable trigger), `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator` (between steps), `StepperContent` (step content panel) 2. **Orientation**: Support both `horizontal` and `vertical` via prop 3. **React context**: Share state (activeStep, orientation, etc.) between sub-components 4. **Export a `useStepper` hook** for external use 5. Create a **demo example** at `examples/stepper-demo.tsx` 6. **Register** both in `ui/_registry.ts` and `examples/_registry.ts` **CRITICAL: Do NOT modify any existing files. Only create new files and ADD registry entries.** ## Existing Patterns to Follow ### Component patterns (from carousel.tsx — the best reference for custom context-based compound components): ```tsx "use client" import * as React from "react" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" // 1. Context type type StepperContextProps = { ... } const StepperContext = React.createContext(null) // 2. Hook with error boundary function useStepper() { const context = React.useContext(StepperContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useStepper must be used within a ") return context } // 3. Function components (not arrow), using data-slot attribute function Stepper({ ... }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperProps) { return (
{children}
 ) } // 4. Named exports export { Stepper, StepperItem, ... , useStepper } ``` ### Orientation pattern (from tabs.tsx): - Parent gets `data-orientation={orientation}` attribute - Children use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/stepper:...` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:...` for responsive styling - Parent has `group/stepper` class for group targeting ### Registry entry pattern: ```typescript // In ui/_registry.ts — append to the existing array: { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], } // In examples/_registry.ts — append to the existing array: { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` ### Example file pattern (from accordion-demo.tsx): ```tsx import { Stepper, StepperItem, ... } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" export default function StepperDemo() { return ( ... ) } ``` ## Design the Stepper Please provide: 1. **Complete component API design** — every sub-component with its props 2. **Context shape** — what state is shared via context 3. **State management** — how activeStep tracking works (controlled + uncontrolled) 4. **Styling approach** — horizontal vs vertical layout using Tailwind classes, group selectors 5. **The stepper should be purely custom** (no Radix primitive) — like the Carousel, it wraps plain divs with context 6. **Detailed Tailwind class strategy** for: - Horizontal layout: steps in a row with separators as horizontal lines between them - Vertical layout: steps in a column with separators as vertical lines between them - Active/completed/inactive step states 7. **Demo example** showing a 3-step wizard Also consider: - Each StepperItem needs a `step` number prop (1-indexed) to identify it - StepperTrigger should be a button element for accessibility - StepperContent should only render when its parent StepperItem's step matches the active step - Support controlled (`value`/`onValueChange`) and uncontrolled (`defaultValue`) patterns - Completed steps (step < activeStep) should look visually distinct from active and upcoming steps

Each StepperItem needs a `step` number prop (1-indexed) to identify it - StepperTrigger should be a button element for accessibility - StepperContent should only render when its parent StepperItem's step matches the active step - Support controlled (`value`/`onValueChange`) and uncontrolled (`defaultValue`) patterns - Completed steps (step < activeStep) should look visually distinct from active and upcoming steps

1	"use client"
2	
3	import * as React from "react"
4	import { type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
5	import { ToggleGroup as ToggleGroupPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
6	
7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
8	import { toggleVariants } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle"
9	
10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(
68	        toggleVariants({
69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )
81	}
82	
83	export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }
84

I have thoroughly explored the codebase. Here is the complete implementation plan for the Stepper compound component. --- ## Stepper Component Implementation Plan ### 1. Files to Create Three files need to be created, and two existing files need registry entries appended: | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` | **NEW** -- The Stepper compound component | | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` | **NEW** -- Demo example for the Stepper | | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` | **APPEND** entry for `stepper` | | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` | **APPEND** entry for `stepper-demo` | No changes to `registry.ts` are needed -- it already spreads `...ui` and `...examples`. --- ### 2. Context Shape ```typescript type StepperContextProps = { activeStep: number orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" setActiveStep: (step: number) => void totalSteps: number isClickable: boolean // Whether triggers can be clicked to navigate } ``` The context is created with `React.createContext(null)` and consumed through a `useStepper` hook that throws if used outside ``, exactly like Carousel's `useCarousel` pattern at line 35-43 of `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx`. A second, inner context is also needed for `StepperItem` to share its `step` number with its descendants (`StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperContent`): ```typescript type StepperItemContextProps = { step: number } const StepperItemContext = React.createContext(null) function useStepperItem() { const context = React.useContext(StepperItemContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useStepperItem must be used within a ") return context } ``` This dual-context approach avoids requiring every child to receive a `step` prop -- only `StepperItem` declares it. --- ### 3. Complete Component API Design #### `Stepper` (container) ```typescript type StepperProps = { orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" // Default: "horizontal" value?: number // Controlled active step (1-indexed) onValueChange?: (step: number) => void // Controlled callback defaultValue?: number // Uncontrolled initial step (default: 1) linear?: boolean // If true, only completed/next step triggers are clickable (default: false) } function Stepper({ orientation = "horizontal", value, onValueChange, defaultValue = 1, linear = false, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperProps) ``` - Renders a `
` with `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation={orientation}`, and `className` including `group/stepper`. - Manages controlled/uncontrolled state for `activeStep` using a standard pattern: if `value` is provided, it is the source of truth and `onValueChange` is called on changes; otherwise, internal `useState(defaultValue)` is used. #### `StepperItem` (individual step) ```typescript type StepperItemProps = { step: number // 1-indexed step identifier (required) } function StepperItem({ step, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperItemProps) ``` - Renders a `
` with `data-slot="stepper-item"`. - Sets `data-state` attribute: `"completed"` if `step < activeStep`, `"active"` if `step === activeStep`, `"inactive"` if `step > activeStep`. - Provides `StepperItemContext` with `{ step }`. - Has flex layout classes that differ by orientation via group selectors. #### `StepperTrigger` (clickable button) ```typescript function StepperTrigger({ className, children, asChild, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"button">) ``` - Renders a `

Design a detailed implementation plan for a new "Stepper" compound component in the shadcn/ui component library. ## Context This is the shadcn/ui monorepo. The component library lives in: - **UI components**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/` (e.g., `tabs.tsx`, `carousel.tsx`, `toggle-group.tsx`) - **Examples**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` (e.g., `accordion-demo.tsx`) - **UI registry**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` - **Examples registry**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - **Main registry**: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts` (imports from sub-registries, no changes needed here since it already spreads `...ui` and `...examples`) ## Requirements Create a **Stepper** compound component for multi-step wizards/onboarding flows. It must: 1. **Sub-components**: `Stepper` (container), `StepperItem` (individual step), `StepperTrigger` (clickable trigger), `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator` (between steps), `StepperContent` (step content panel) 2. **Orientation**: Support both `horizontal` and `vertical` via prop 3. **React context**: Share state (activeStep, orientation, etc.) between sub-components 4. **Export a `useStepper` hook** for external use 5. Create a **demo example** at `examples/stepper-demo.tsx` 6. **Register** both in `ui/_registry.ts` and `examples/_registry.ts` **CRITICAL: Do NOT modify any existing files. Only create new files and ADD registry entries.** ## Existing Patterns to Follow ### Component patterns (from carousel.tsx — the best reference for custom context-based compound components): ```tsx "use client" import * as React from "react" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" // 1. Context type type StepperContextProps = { ... } const StepperContext = React.createContext(null) // 2. Hook with error boundary function useStepper() { const context = React.useContext(StepperContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useStepper must be used within a ") return context } // 3. Function components (not arrow), using data-slot attribute function Stepper({ ... }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperProps) { return (
{children}
 ) } // 4. Named exports export { Stepper, StepperItem, ... , useStepper } ``` ### Orientation pattern (from tabs.tsx): - Parent gets `data-orientation={orientation}` attribute - Children use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/stepper:...` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:...` for responsive styling - Parent has `group/stepper` class for group targeting ### Registry entry pattern: ```typescript // In ui/_registry.ts — append to the existing array: { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], } // In examples/_registry.ts — append to the existing array: { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` ### Example file pattern (from accordion-demo.tsx): ```tsx import { Stepper, StepperItem, ... } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" export default function StepperDemo() { return ( ... ) } ``` ## Design the Stepper Please provide: 1. **Complete component API design** — every sub-component with its props 2. **Context shape** — what state is shared via context 3. **State management** — how activeStep tracking works (controlled + uncontrolled) 4. **Styling approach** — horizontal vs vertical layout using Tailwind classes, group selectors 5. **The stepper should be purely custom** (no Radix primitive) — like the Carousel, it wraps plain divs with context 6. **Detailed Tailwind class strategy** for: - Horizontal layout: steps in a row with separators as horizontal lines between them - Vertical layout: steps in a column with separators as vertical lines between them - Active/completed/inactive step states 7. **Demo example** showing a 3-step wizard Also consider: - Each StepperItem needs a `step` number prop (1-indexed) to identify it - StepperTrigger should be a button element for accessibility - StepperContent should only render when its parent StepperItem's step matches the active step - Support controlled (`value`/`onValueChange`) and uncontrolled (`defaultValue`) patterns - Completed steps (step < activeStep) should look visually distinct from active and upcoming steps

Files to Create Three files need to be created, and two existing files need registry entries appended: | File | Purpose | |------|---------| | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` | **NEW** -- The Stepper compound component | | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` | **NEW** -- Demo example for the Stepper | | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` | **APPEND** entry for `stepper` | | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` | **APPEND** entry for `stepper-demo` | No changes to `registry.ts` are needed -- it already spreads `...ui` and `...examples`. --- ### 2. Context Shape ```typescript type StepperContextProps = { activeStep: number orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" setActiveStep: (step: number) => void totalSteps: number isClickable: boolean // Whether triggers can be clicked to navigate } ``` The context is created with `React.createContext(null)` and consumed through a `useStepper` hook that throws if used outside ``, exactly like Carousel's `useCarousel` pattern at line 35-43 of `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775504481808-x1wj7g/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx`. A second, inner context is also needed for `StepperItem` to share its `step` number with its descendants (`StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperContent`): ```typescript type StepperItemContextProps = { step: number } const StepperItemContext = React.createContext(null) function useStepperItem() { const context = React.useContext(StepperItemContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useStepperItem must be used within a ") return context } ``` This dual-context approach avoids requiring every child to receive a `step` prop -- only `StepperItem` declares it. --- ### 3. Complete Component API Design #### `Stepper` (container) ```typescript type StepperProps = { orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" // Default: "horizontal" value?: number // Controlled active step (1-indexed) onValueChange?: (step: number) => void // Controlled callback defaultValue?: number // Uncontrolled initial step (default: 1) linear?: boolean // If true, only completed/next step triggers are clickable (default: false) } function Stepper({ orientation = "horizontal", value, onValueChange, defaultValue = 1, linear = false, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperProps) ``` - Renders a `
` with `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation={orientation}`, and `className` including `group/stepper`. - Manages controlled/uncontrolled state for `activeStep` using a standard pattern: if `value` is provided, it is the source of truth and `onValueChange` is called on changes; otherwise, internal `useState(defaultValue)` is used. #### `StepperItem` (individual step) ```typescript type StepperItemProps = { step: number // 1-indexed step identifier (required) } function StepperItem({ step, className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & StepperItemProps) ``` - Renders a `
` with `data-slot="stepper-item"`. - Sets `data-state` attribute: `"completed"` if `step < activeStep`, `"active"` if `step === activeStep`, `"inactive"` if `step > activeStep`. - Provides `StepperItemContext` with `{ step }`. - Has flex layout classes that differ by orientation via group selectors. #### `StepperTrigger` (clickable button) ```typescript function StepperTrigger({ className, children, asChild, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"button">) ``` - Renders a `

# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component ## Context The shadcn/ui component library needs a **Stepper** compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. No stepper currently exists. The implementation follows the same patterns as existing compound components (Carousel for React context, Tabs for orientation support, ToggleGroup for context-shared variants). **Constraint:** Do NOT modify any existing component files. Only create new files and append registry entries. --- ## Files to Create / Modify | Action | File | |--------|------| | **CREATE** | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` | | **CREATE** | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` | | **APPEND** | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (line 705, before closing `]`) | | **APPEND** | `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` (line 2651, before closing `]`) | No changes to `registry.ts` — it already spreads `...ui` and `...examples`. --- ## Step 1: Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Architecture Two React contexts: - **`StepperContext`** — shared by the root `Stepper`, provides `activeStep`, `setActiveStep`, `orientation`, `linear` - **`StepperItemContext`** — provided by each `StepperItem`, shares its `step` number with descendants ### Context Types ```typescript type StepperContextProps = { activeStep: number setActiveStep: (step: number) => void orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" linear: boolean } type StepperItemContextProps = { step: number } ``` Both created with `React.createContext<...Props | null>(null)` and consumed via hooks that throw when used outside their provider (following `carousel.tsx` lines 33-43). ### State Management (Controlled + Uncontrolled) ```typescript function Stepper({ value, onValueChange, defaultValue = 1, ... }) { const [internalStep, setInternalStep] = React.useState(defaultValue) const isControlled = value !== undefined const activeStep = isControlled ? value : internalStep const setActiveStep = React.useCallback((step: number) => { if (!isControlled) setInternalStep(step) onValueChange?.(step) }, [isControlled, onValueChange]) // ...provide via context } ``` ### Sub-components (7 total + 1 public hook) | Component | Element | `data-slot` | Key behavior | |-----------|---------|-------------|-------------| | `Stepper` | `
` | `stepper` | Root container. Provides `StepperContext`. Has `group/stepper`, `data-orientation`. Supports `orientation`, `value`/`onValueChange`/`defaultValue`, `linear` props. | | `StepperItem` | `
` | `stepper-item` | Wraps one step. Accepts `step: number` prop. Provides `StepperItemContext`. Sets `data-state="completed" \| "active" \| "inactive"`. Has `group/stepper-item`. | | `StepperTrigger` | `

content.text:
The plan is complete. Here's a summary of what will be implemented:

**New Stepper compound component** with 7 sub-components (`Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent`) plus a `useStepper` hook.

**Key design decisions:**
- **Dual React context** — `StepperContext` for global state (activeStep, orientation), `StepperItemContext` for per-step state (step number)
- **Controlled + uncontrolled** — supports `value`/`onValueChange` or `defaultValue`
- **Orientation via data attributes** — uses `group/stepper` + `group-data-[orientation=...]` selectors (matches Tabs pattern)
- **Step states via `data-state`** — `completed` | `active` | `inactive` drives styling through group selectors
- **`StepperContent` takes its own `step` prop** — placed as direct child of `Stepper` (not inside `StepperItem`) so horizontal content wraps below the indicator row
- **`StepperSeparator` inside `StepperItem`** — inherits step from `StepperItemContext`, no explicit prop needed
- **No new dependencies** — only uses `lucide-react` (already in the project) for the check icon

**4 file operations:** 2 new files created, 2 registry entries appended.

3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the
4	// apps/v4/public/schema/registry-item.json file.
5	
6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),
69	    utils: z.string(),
70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.
79	export const workspaceConfigSchema = z.record(configSchema)
80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])
192	
193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
194	
195	// Helper type for registry:base items specifically.
196	export type RegistryBaseItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:base" }>
197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>
200	
201	export const registrySchema = z.object({
202	  name: z.string(),
203	  homepage: z.string(),
204	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema),
205	})
206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(

213	    name: z.string(),
214	    label: z.string(),
215	  })
216	)
217	
218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(
219	  z.string(),
220	  z.record(z.string(), z.string())
221	)
222	
223	export const registryBaseColorSchema = z.object({
224	  inlineColors: z.object({

226	    dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),

229	  cssVarsV4: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
230	  inlineColorsTemplate: z.string(),
231	  cssVarsTemplate: z.string(),
232	})
233	
234	export const registryResolvedItemsTreeSchema = registryItemCommonSchema
235	  .pick({

237	    devDependencies: true,
238	    files: true,
239	    tailwind: true,
240	    cssVars: true,

242	    envVars: true,

255

29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const
35	
36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}
